title: age-group — outbound webhooks
description: Tenant admins can subscribe to age-group-lifecycle events via the webhook module (shared/webhooks, Wave 4). This blueprint declares the event types this module publishes. Payload shapes mirror the corresponding entries in `events.json` — the webhook module wraps them in the standard envelope.

event_types.description: New AgeGroup row created. Payload includes name, slug, sport_key, min_age, max_age, cutoff_date_kind, is_youth, is_default, is_seeded, source.
AgeGroup mutated (excluding the four dedicated events below). Payload lists `changed_fields`.
min_age_inclusive OR max_age_inclusive mutated. Audit-critical. Payload includes { min_age: {before, after}, max_age: {before, after}, is_youth_flip }.
The is_default flag flipped within a (tenant, organization, sport_key) tuple. Payload includes from_age_group_id + to_age_group_id.
AgeGroup soft-deleted (archived). Payload includes reason + hard_delete_scheduled_for.
Archived AgeGroup transitioned back to active.
AgeGroup soft-deleted OR hard-deleted (payload carries mode).

The tenant's default catalog was seeded on TenantProvisioned. Fires exactly once per tenant lifetime under normal operations. Payload includes { tenant_id, age_group_ids: [10 IDs], default_age_group_id, seeded_by, catalog_version }.

notes: Every payload is signed with the tenant's webhook secret (HMAC-SHA256) per the shared webhook contract.
AgeGroup rows carry no PII — payloads are safe to route to any tenant-approved webhook destination.

The bounds_changed + cutoff_changed events do NOT include the count of affected Athletes in the payload — that count is a downstream module concern (Wave 3+). Consumers who need it should call the Athlete module's own API.
